_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to formulate, refine, and use sophisticated historical questions to inform and structure our investigations.

_source.w: We are learning to formulate, refine, and use sophisticated historical questions to inform and structure our investigations.
y: Sophisticated questions are the hallmark of mature historical inquiry — they drive investigations toward depth rather than breadth.
Refining questions in response to evidence demonstrates intellectual flexibility and growth.
This metacognitive skill — questioning our own questions — underpins all rigorous academic work.
eg: Formulating a multi-layered historical question that addresses causation, significance, and perspectives.
Refining our question as initial research reveals new complexities or redirects our focus.
Using sub-questions to structure a comprehensive investigation.
Evaluating our questions against criteria — Are they analytical? Researchable? Do they lead to sustained argument?
Comparing our questions with published historical research questions and learning from the comparison.
Reflecting on how the evolution of our questions mirrors our deepening understanding.
